title: Delta Spec
description: Required output format when reuse Level 1-4 is chosen. Produced BEFORE any use_figma call, as part of the RULE 19 wireframe gate. Describes what to preserve, replace, add, and remove relative to the base canonical.

baseCanonical.description: ID from canonical-index.json (Tier 2 node ID preferred, Tier 1 shipped ID as fallback). Example: '750:174925' or 'shipped-outage-list'

baseCanonicalName.description: Human-readable name of the chosen canonical. Example: 'Outage List Overview'

reuseLevel.description: 1=Exact, 2=Similar, 3=Floorplan, 4=Component, 5=New. Level 5 means no delta — build from scratch.

similarityScore.description: Score 0-100. Formula: floorplan(50%) + regions(30%) + components(20%). Threshold: >=85 clone direct, 60-84 clone+delta, <60 Level 5.

scoreBreakdown.description: Optional breakdown of how the score was computed

preserve.description: Regions and components to keep exactly as-is from the canonical. Example: ['ShellBar', 'DynamicPageTitle', 'PaginationBar']

replace.description: Content to inject into preserved structure. Keys are slot names; values describe new content.

add.description: SAP components or regions to add that don't exist in the canonical. Example: ['FilterBar', 'ProgressIndicator']

remove.description: Regions or components from the canonical to omit in the new build. Example: ['Calendar', 'MessageStrip']

swapComponents.description: Component substitutions — replace one SAP component type with another

newWork.description: Percentage of work that is genuinely new (100 - similarityScore). Aim to minimize.

notes.description: Any important context about the delta — why certain choices were made

examples.notes: High similarity — only content and filter bar differ
Similar floorplan, different toolbar and filter structure
